steps.title: Identify what needs evidence
steps.content: The claim rests on a specific fact: consumer excitement fades quickly. Any supporting evidence must directly show this fading effect.

steps.title: Match evidence to fading excitement
steps.content: Choice C shows that products announced far in advance of release have low consumer excitement—directly proving that enthusiasm decreases over time. The other choices discuss product preference, brand loyalty, and information type, but none demonstrate that excitement itself fades. That matches choice C — C is correct.
